items.title: USDA posts state-by-state SNAP food-restriction waivers with 2026–2028 start dates
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP Food Restriction Waivers list shows multiple states restricting purchases of items like soda and candy, with target implementation dates ranging from 01/01/26 (e.g., Indiana, Iowa, Nebraska, Utah, West Virginia) through 02/01/28 (Nevada). The USDA table includes additional mid-2026 start dates such as Arkansas (07/01/26) and Tennessee (07/31/26), and notes several waivers cover sugar-sweetened beverages and/or candy.

items.title: CMS: New Chief Actuary certification requirement for Medicaid 1115 waivers starts Jan. 1, 2027
items.summary: In a June 11 State Medicaid Director letter, CMS said that beginning 01/01/27 it cannot approve a Medicaid section 1115 demonstration (or an amendment/renewal) unless the CMS Chief Actuary certifies the project will not increase federal Medicaid spending. CMS said the requirement applies to all states, D.C., and territories, and the letter notes this new statutory requirement does not apply to CHIP section 1115 demonstrations.

items.title: HUD opens $193 million youth-homelessness funding opportunity; applications due Aug. 10, 2026
items.summary: HUD announced $193 million for youth homelessness work, including a Youth Homelessness NOFO that HUD says competes $144 million for 26–50 new Youth Homelessness Demonstration Program (YHDP) communities and $49 million for Youth Homelessness System Improvement (YHSI) grants. HUD says applications are due 08/10/26.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: SSA 2026 Trustees Report: Combined trust funds projected to pay full benefits until 2034
items.summary: SSA said the combined OASI and DI trust funds are projected to have dedicated revenue to pay all scheduled benefits and associated administrative costs until 2034, after which 83% of benefits would be payable. SSA also reported the combined trust fund reserves declined by $160 billion in 2025 to $2.56 trillion, and that Social Security had 70 million beneficiaries at the end of 2025.

items.title: IRS details bigger employer child care credit for tax year 2026 and later (up to $500k/$600k)
items.summary: The IRS says the amended Section 45F employer-provided child care credit cap rises from $150,000 to $500,000 (or $600,000 for eligible small businesses), for amounts paid or incurred after 12/31/25. The IRS also says the credit rate on qualified child care expenditures increases from 25% to 40% (50% for eligible small businesses), and notes a 2026 eligible-small-business gross-receipts test threshold of $32,000,000 (average annual gross receipts over the prior five years).

items.title: USDA posts 2026–2027 WIC income guidelines (185% of poverty; family of 4: $61,050/year)
items.summary: USDA’s WIC FAQ states that for the period 07/01/26 to 06/30/27, income eligibility is at or below 185% of the U.S. poverty income guidelines, and lists example thresholds such as $61,050 per year for a family of 4 (or $5,088 per month). The same table lists $29,526 per year for a family of 1 and adds $10,508 per additional family member.

items.title: FAFSA 2026–27: Federal deadline is June 30, 2027 (form available starting Oct. 1, 2025)
items.summary: The 2026–27 FAFSA PDF says you can submit the FAFSA for federal aid as early as possible but no earlier than 10/01/25. It also states the Department of Education must receive the application no later than 06/30/27.
items.source: Federal Student Aid (U.S. Department of Education)
